categories.name: كَلِمَاتٌ تَتَعَلَّقُ بِالْكَمِّيَّةِ
categories.nameEn: Words related to quantity or counting

items.verse: لِّيَعْلَمَ أَن قَدْ أَبْلَغُوا۟ رِسَـٰلَـٰتِ رَبِّهِمْ وَأَحَاطَ بِمَا لَدَيْهِمْ وَأَحْصَىٰ كُلَّ شَىْءٍ عَدَدًۢا
items.verseEn: That he [i.e., Muḥammad (ﷺ)] may know that they have conveyed the messages of their Lord; and He has encompassed whatever is with them and has enumerated all things in number.
Half of it - or subtract from it a little
And We will inherit him [in] what he mentions, and he will come to Us alone.

items.verse: وَقَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ لَوْلَا نُزِّلَ عَلَيْهِ ٱلْقُرْءَانُ جُمْلَةًۭ وَٰحِدَةًۭ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ لِنُثَبِّتَ بِهِۦ فُؤَادَكَ ۖ وَرَتَّلْنَـٰهُ تَرْتِيلًۭا
items.verseEn: And those who disbelieve say, "Why was the Qur’ān not revealed to him all at once?" Thus [it is] that We may strengthen thereby your heart. And We have spaced it distinctly.

categories.name: كَلِمَاتٌ مِنْ قِصَّةِ أَصْحَابِ السَّبْتِ
categories.nameEn: Words from the story of the Sabbath-breakers

items.verse: وَسْـَٔلْهُمْ عَنِ ٱلْقَرْيَةِ ٱلَّتِى كَانَتْ حَاضِرَةَ ٱلْبَحْرِ إِذْ يَعْدُونَ فِى ٱلسَّبْتِ إِذْ تَأْتِيهِمْ حِيتَانُهُمْ يَوْمَ سَبْتِهِمْ شُرَّعًۭا وَيَوْمَ لَا يَسْبِتُونَ ۙ لَا تَأْتِيهِمْ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ نَبْلُوهُم بِمَا كَانُوا۟ يَفْسُقُونَ
items.verseEn: And ask them about the town that was by the sea - when they transgressed in [the matter of] the sabbath - when their fish came to them openly on their sabbath day, and the day they had no sabbath they did not come to them. Thus did We give them trial because they were defiantly disobedient.

items.verse: وَإِذْ قَالَتْ أُمَّةٌۭ مِّنْهُمْ لِمَ تَعِظُونَ قَوْمًا ۙ ٱللَّهُ مُهْلِكُهُمْ أَوْ مُعَذِّبُهُمْ عَذَابًۭا شَدِيدًۭا ۖ قَالُوا۟ مَعْذِرَةً إِلَىٰ رَبِّكُمْ وَلَعَلَّهُمْ يَتَّقُونَ
items.verseEn: And when a community among them said, "Why do you advise [or warn] a people whom Allāh is [about] to destroy or to punish with a severe punishment?" they [the advisors] said, "To be absolved before your Lord and perhaps they may fear Him."

items.verse: فَلَمَّا نَسُوا۟ مَا ذُكِّرُوا۟ بِهِۦٓ أَنجَيْنَا ٱلَّذِينَ يَنْهَوْنَ عَنِ ٱلسُّوٓءِ وَأَخَذْنَا ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا۟ بِعَذَابٍۭ بَـِٔيسٍۭ بِمَا كَانُوا۟ يَفْسُقُونَ
items.verseEn: And when they [i.e., those advised] forgot that by which they had been reminded, We saved those who had forbidden evil and seized those who wronged, with a wretched punishment, because they were defiantly disobeying.

items.verse: وَلَقَدْ عَلِمْتُمُ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱعْتَدَوْا۟ مِنكُمْ فِى ٱلسَّبْتِ فَقُلْنَا لَهُمْ كُونُوا۟ قِرَدَةً خَـٰسِـِٔينَ
items.verseEn: And you had already known about those who transgressed among you concerning the sabbath, and We said to them, "Be apes, despised."

categories.name: كَلِمَاتٌ وَقَعَتْ نَائِبَ فَاعِلٍ
categories.nameEn: Words that are the subject of a passive verb (nāʾib al-fāʿil)

items.verseEn: And when the wild beasts are gathered

items.verse: يُعْرَفُ ٱلْمُجْرِمُونَ بِسِيمَـٰهُمْ فَيُؤْخَذُ بِٱلنَّوَٰصِى وَٱلْأَقْدَامِ
items.verseEn: The criminals will be known by their marks, and they will be seized by the forelocks and the feet.

items.verse: يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ أَوْفُوا۟ بِٱلْعُقُودِ ۚ أُحِلَّتْ لَكُم بَهِيمَةُ ٱلْأَنْعَـٰمِ إِلَّا مَا يُتْلَىٰ عَلَيْكُمْ غَيْرَ مُحِلِّى ٱلصَّيْدِ وَأَنتُمْ حُرُمٌ ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يَحْكُمُ مَا يُرِيدُ
items.verseEn: O you who have believed, fulfill [all] contracts. Lawful for you are the animals of grazing livestock except for that which is recited to you [in this Qur’ān] - hunting not being permitted while you are in the state of iḥrām. Indeed, Allāh ordains what He intends.

items.verse: وَيَوْمَ يُحْشَرُ أَعْدَآءُ ٱللَّهِ إِلَى ٱلنَّارِ فَهُمْ يُوزَعُونَ
items.verseEn: And [mention, O Muḥammad], the Day when the enemies of Allāh will be gathered to the Fire while they are [driven], assembled in rows,

categories.name: مَعْبُودَاتٌ بَاطِلَةٌ ذُكِرَتْ فِي الْقُرْآنِ
categories.nameEn: Objects of false worship mentioned in the Quran

items.verse: وَقَالُوا۟ لَا تَذَرُنَّ ءَالِهَتَكُمْ وَلَا تَذَرُنَّ وَدًّۭا وَلَا سُوَاعًۭا وَلَا يَغُوثَ وَيَعُوقَ وَنَسْرًۭا
items.verseEn: And said, 'Never leave your gods and never leave Wadd or Suwāʿ or Yaghūth and Yaʿūq and Nasr.'
So have you considered al-Lāt and al-ʿUzzā?
And Manāt, the third - the other one?

items.verse: وَإِذْ وَٰعَدْنَا مُوسَىٰٓ أَرْبَعِينَ لَيْلَةًۭ ثُمَّ ٱتَّخَذْتُمُ ٱلْعِجْلَ مِنۢ بَعْدِهِۦ وَأَنتُمْ ظَـٰلِمُونَ
items.verseEn: And [recall] when We made an appointment with Moses for forty nights. Then you took [for worship] the calf after him [i.e., his departure], while you were wrongdoers.

harf.hint: They can be found in the heavens, the earth, and within yourself.
harf.arabicVerse: وَمِنْ ءَايَـٰتِهِۦٓ أَنْ خَلَقَكُم مِّن تُرَابٍۢ ثُمَّ إِذَآ أَنتُم بَشَرٌۭ تَنتَشِرُونَ
harf.verse: 30:20 — And of His signs is that He created you from dust; then, suddenly you were human beings dispersing [throughout the earth].

deduction.title: The Shadow in the City of Light
deduction.intro: In the heart of a community built on faith and brotherhood, a faction lurked in the shadows. Their words were smooth like honey, but their hearts held a bitter poison. They prayed alongside the righteous, yet conspired against them in secret, their presence a constant test for the sincere. Who are these people?
clues: We lived in the same city as the final Prophet, yet our allegiance was a lie.
We would attend gatherings of faith, only to turn away and mock what was said.
When the call for sacrifice came, we would invent excuses to avoid hardship.
We used our eloquent speech and sworn oaths to deceive the believers.
Our leader was a prominent figure, but his inner self was exposed by divine revelation.
An entire chapter of the Quran is named for us, detailing our deceit and warning the believers about us.

identity.label: Who Are We?
options: The Companions of the Cave
The Ansar of Madinah
The Hypocrites (Al-Munafiqun)
The People of the Book who believed
The Muhajirun from Makkah

trial.label: What Was Our Primary Trait?
options: Fleeing persecution to a cave
Professing faith while hiding disbelief
Breaking the Sabbath by fishing
Building a golden calf for worship
Plotting to kill a prophet in a well

location.label: What Was Our Key Method?
options: A miraculous she-camel
A staff that turned into a serpent
A fortified wall of iron and copper
Deceptive Oaths and Rumors
A flock of birds carrying baked clay

outcome.label: What Was Our Promised Fate?
options: Being turned into apes
Being drowned in a great flood
Promised the lowest depths of the Fire
Preserved as a sign for humanity
Honored in Paradise for their patience
outcome.answer: Promised the lowest depths of the Fire

deduction.arabic: إِذَا جَآءَكَ ٱلْمُنَـٰفِقُونَ قَالُوا۟ نَشْهَدُ إِنَّكَ لَرَسُولُ ٱللَّهِ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ يَعْلَمُ إِنَّكَ لَرَسُولُهُۥ وَٱللَّهُ يَشْهَدُ إِنَّ ٱلْمُنَـٰفِقِينَ لَكَـٰذِبُونَ
deduction.verse: When the hypocrites come to you, [O Muḥammad], they say, "We testify that you are the Messenger of Allāh." And Allāh knows that you are His Messenger, and Allāh testifies that the hypocrites are liars. (63:1)

scramble.hint: This verse describes a selfless act of charity, highlighting a key quality of the righteous.
scramble.arabic: وَيُطْعِمُونَ ٱلطَّعَامَ عَلَىٰ حُبِّهِۦ مِسْكِينًۭا وَيَتِيمًۭا وَأَسِيرًا
scramble.verseEn: And they give food in spite of love for it to the needy, the orphan, and the captive,
